sections.label: Section 1
sections.original: ὦ τοῦ στρατηγήσαντος ἐν Τροίᾳ ποτὲ
sections.translation: Son of him who once commanded our forces at Troy , son of Agamemnon !— now you may survey all that your heart has desired for so long . There is the ancient Argos of your yearning ,

sections.label: Section 5
sections.translation: that consecrated land from which the gad-fly drove the daughter of Inachus ; there , Orestes , is the Lycean market place , named from the wolf-slaying god ; there on the left is Hera ’ s famous temple ; and in this place to which we have come , know that you see Mycenae , the rich in gold ,

sections.label: Section 10
sections.translation: and here the house of Pelops ’ heirs , so often stained with bloodshed . Long ago from here , away from the murder of your father , I carried you for her whose blood is yours , your sister , and saved you and reared you up to manhood to be the avenger of your murdered father .

sections.label: Section 15
sections.original: νῦν οὖν , Ὀρέστα καὶ σὺ φίλτατε ξένων
sections.translation: Now , therefore , Orestes , and you , best of allies , Pylades , our plan of action must be quickly laid ; for look , already the sun ’ s bright ray is stirring the birds ’ songs into clarity , and the kindly darkness of the stars is spent .

sections.label: Section 20
sections.original: πρὶν οὖν τινʼ ἀνδρῶν ἐξοδοιπορεῖν στέγης ,
sections.translation: Before , then , anyone comes out from the house , we must make our plans , since we are at the point where it is no longer opportune to hesitate , but it is the moment for action .

sections.label: Section 23
sections.original: ὦ φίλτατʼ ἀνδρῶν προσπόλων , ὥς μοι σαφῆ
sections.translation: True friend and follower , how well you prove your loyalty to our house !

sections.label: Section 25
sections.original: ὥσπερ γὰρ ἵππος εὐγενής , κἂν ᾖ γέρων ,
sections.translation: Just as a thoroughbred mount , even if advanced in years , does not lose courage in danger , but pricks up his ears , so you speed us forward and follow in the first ranks . I will tell you , then , what I have determined .

sections.label: Section 30
sections.original: ὀξεῖαν ἀκοὴν τοῖς ἐμοῖς λόγοις διδούς ,
sections.translation:
Listen closely to my words and correct me , if I miss the mark in any way .
When I went to the Pythian oracle to learn how I might avenge my father on his murderers ,

sections.label: Section 35
sections.original: χρῇ μοι τοιαῦθʼ ὁ Φοῖβος ὧν πεύσει τάχα ·
sections.translation: Phoebus gave me the commandment which you will now hear : that alone , and by stealth , without the aid of arms or large numbers , I should carry off my right hand ’ s just slaughters . Accordingly , since I received this divine declaration , you must go into that house there

sections.label: Section 40
sections.original: δόμων ἔσω τῶνδʼ , ἴσθι πᾶν τὸ δρώμενον ,
sections.translation: when opportunity gives you entrance , and learn all that is happening , so that you may report to us out of sure knowledge . Your age and the lapse of time will prevent them from recognizing you ; they will never suspect who you are with that silvered hair . Let your story be that you are a Phocian stranger

sections.label: Section 45
sections.original: Φωκέως παρʼ ἀνδρὸς Φανοτέως ἥκων · ὃ γὰρ
sections.translation: sent by Phanoteus , since he is the greatest of their allies . Tell them , and affirm it with your oath , that Orestes has perished by a fatal chance , hurled at the Pythian games

sections.label: Section 50
sections.original: δίφρων κυλισθείς · ὧδʼ ὁ μῦθος ἑστάτω .
sections.translation: from his speeding chariot . Let that be the substance of your message . Meanwhile , we will first crown my father ’ s tomb as the god ordered with libations and the luxuriant tribute of a severed lock ; then we will return here , bearing in our hands an urn of hammered bronze

sections.label: Section 55
sections.original: ὃ καὶ σὺ θάμνοις οἶσθά που κεκρυμμένον ,
sections.translation: — now hidden , as you no doubt know , in the brushwood — so that we may gladden them with the false tidings that this body of mine exists no more , but has been consumed with fire and reduced to ashes . How does it hurt me , when by feigned death

sections.label: Section 60
sections.translation: I find true life and win renown ? No word is ill-omened , I trust , if it yields gain . For often before now I have seen clever men die in false report ; then , when they return home , they are held in greater honor .

sections.label: Section 65
sections.original: ὡς κἄμʼ ἐπαυχῶ τῆσδε τῆς φήμης ἄπο
sections.translation:
And so for myself I trust that as a result of this rumor I , too , shall live , shining down like a star upon my enemies .
But you , O my fatherland and native gods of my soil , receive me with good fortune in this journey , and you also , house of my ancestors ,

sections.label: Section 70
sections.translation:
since I come by divine mandate to cleanse you as justice demands . Do not dismiss me from this land in dishonor , but grant that I may rule over my possessions and restore my house !
I have said enough . Go now , old one , and take care to watch over your task .

sections.label: Section 75
sections.original: νὼ δʼ ἔξιμεν · καιρὸς γάρ , ὅσπερ ἀνδράσιν
sections.translation: The two of us will depart ; for so opportunity bids , chief ruler of every enterprise for men .

sections.label: Section 77
sections.translation: ah , me , ah , joyless me !

sections.label: Section 78
sections.original: καὶ μὴν θυρῶν ἔδοξα προσπόλων τινὸς
sections.translation: Listen , my son ; from the doors of the house , I think , came the sound of some serving girl moaning inside .

sections.label: Section 80
sections.original: ἆρʼ ἐστὶν ἡ δύστηνος Ἠλέκτρα · θέλεις
sections.translation: Can it be my joyless Electra ? Shall we remain here and listen to her cries ?

sections.label: Section 82
sections.original: ἥκιστα · μηδὲν πρόσθεν ἢ τὰ Λοξίου
sections.translation: No , no ; before all else , let us strive to obey the commands of Loxias and from them make a fair beginning by pouring libations to your father . For such actions bring

sections.label: Section 85
sections.original: νίκην τʼ ἐφʼ ἡμῖν καὶ κράτος τῶν δρωμένων .
sections.translation: victory within our grasp and give us mastery in all our doings . Exeunt Paedagogus on the spectators left , Orestes and Pylades on the right .

sections.label: Section 86
sections.translation: O you pure sunlight , and you air , light ’ s equal partner over earth , how often have you heard the chords of my laments

sections.label: Section 90
sections.translation: and the thudding blows against this bloodied breast at the time of gloomy night ’ s leaving off ! My accursed bed in that house of suffering there knows well already how I observe my night-long rites — how often I bewail my miserable

sections.label: Section 95
sections.original: πατέρʼ , ὃν κατὰ μὲν βάρβαρον αἶαν
sections.translation: father , whom bloody Ares did not welcome with deadly gifts in a foreign land , but my mother and her bedfellow Aegisthus split his head with murderous axe , just as woodmen chop an oak .

sections.label: Section 100
sections.translation: And for this crime no pitying cry bursts from any lips but mine , when you , Father , have died a death so cruel and so deserving of pity !

sections.label: Section 103
sections.translation: But never will I end from cries and bitter lamentation ,

sections.label: Section 105
sections.translation: while I look on the stars ’ glistening flashes or on this light of day . No , like the nightingale , slayer of her offspring , I will wail without ceasing , and cry aloud to all here at the doors of my father .

sections.label: Section 110
sections.translation: O House of Hades and Persephone ! O Hermes of the shades ! O potent Curse , and you fearsome daughters of the gods , the Erinyes , who take note when a life is unjustly taken , when a marriage-bed is thievishly dishonored ,

sections.label: Section 115
sections.translation: come , help me , bring vengeance for the murder of my father and send me my brother . I no longer have the strength to hold up alone against

sections.label: Section 120
sections.translation: the load of grief that crushes me .

sections.label: Section 1.121
sections.translation: Ah , Electra , child of a most wretched mother , why are you always wasting away in this unsated mourning for Agamemnon , who long ago was godlessly

sections.label: Section 1.125
sections.translation: ensnared in your false mother ’ s wiles and betrayed by her corrupt hand ? May the one who did that perish , if I may speak such a curse without breaking the gods ’ laws .

sections.label: Section 1.130
sections.translation: you have come to relieve me in my troubles . I know and feel it : it does not escape me . Still I cannot leave this task undone , nor abandon this mourning for my poor father . Ah , friends whose love responds to mine in every mood ,

sections.label: Section 1.135
sections.translation: allow me to rave as I am , oh , please , I beg you !

sections.label: Section 1.137
sections.original: ἀλλʼ οὔτοι τόν γʼ ἐξ Ἀΐδα
sections.translation: But never by weeping nor by prayer will you resurrect your father from the pool of Hades which receives all men .

sections.label: Section 1.140
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἀπὸ τῶν μετρίων ἐπʼ ἀμήχανον
sections.translation: No , by grieving without end and beyond due limits you will find cureless misery and your own ruin ; in these actions there is no deliverance from evils . Tell us , why do you pursue such suffering ?

sections.label: Section 1.145
sections.translation: Foolish is the child who forgets a parent ’ s piteous death . No , closer to my heart is the mourner who eternally wails , Itys , Itys , that bird mad with grief , the messenger of Zeus .

sections.label: Section 1.150
sections.original: ἰὼ παντλάμων Νιόβα , σὲ δʼ ἔγωγε νέμω θεόν ,
sections.translation: Ah , all-suffering Niobe , you I count divine , since you weep forever in your rocky tomb !

sections.label: Section 2.155
sections.translation: though you face it with less restraint than those girls inside , Chrysothemis and Iphianassa , whose parents and blood you share . They still live , as he , too , lives , sorrowing in his secluded youth ,

sections.label: Section 2.160
sections.translation: yet happy in that this famous realm of the Mycenaeans shall one day receive him as a noble lord , if with the blessing of Zeus ’ s escort he , Orestes , returns to this land .

sections.label: Section 2.164
sections.original: ὅν γʼ ἐγὼ ἀκάματα προσμένουσʼ , ἄτεκνος ,
sections.translation: Yes , I await him with unwearied longing ,

sections.label: Section 2.165
sections.translation: as I walk my sad path from day to day childless and unwed , bathed in tears , bearing that endless doom of evils . But he forgets all that he has suffered and learned .

sections.label: Section 2.170
sections.translation: What message comes to me that is not proven false ? He constantly desires to be with us , but though he desires it , he does not choose to appear .

sections.label: Section 2.175
sections.translation: is still mighty , and he sees and rules all . Leave your oversharp anger to him ; be neither excessively hostile to those you hate , nor forgetful of them , since Time is a god who brings ease .

sections.label: Section 2.180
sections.translation: Neither the son of Agamemnon , who dwells by Crisa ’ s cattle-feeding shore nor the god who reigns beside Acheron is unmindful of you .

sections.label: Section 2.185
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἐμὲ μὲν ὁ πολὺς ἀπολέλοιπεν ἤδη
sections.translation: But the best part of life has passed away leaving me in hopelessness , and I have no strength left . I waste away without children and have no loving husband to champion me , but like some despised foreign slave ,

sections.label: Section 2.190
sections.translation: I serve in the halls of my father , wrapped in shabby garments and standing to eat scanty meals .

sections.label: Section 3.193
sections.translation: Mournful was the voice heard at his return , and mournful the voice amidst your father ’ s reclining banquet

sections.label: Section 3.195
sections.translation: when the straight , swift blow of the bronze-jawed axe was sped against him . Deceit was the plotter , Lust the slayer , two dread parents of a dreadful

sections.label: Section 3.200
sections.translation: phantom , whether it was god or mortal that did this deed .

sections.label: Section 3.201
sections.translation: O that hated day , hated beyond all that have come to me ; O that night , the terrible grief of that unutterable feast , the shameless death-strokes

sections.label: Section 3.205
sections.translation: my father saw from their twin hands , hands which took my life captive by treachery , which cast me to ruin ! May the great god of Olympus

sections.label: Section 3.210
sections.translation: create for them sufferings in penalty , and may they never have enjoyment of their splendor since they have committed such crimes !

sections.label: Section 3.213
sections.translation: Be advised to say no more ; do you not see by what actions

sections.label: Section 3.215
sections.translation: under the present circumstances you plunge so shamefully into self-made miseries ? You have far excelled in achieving misfortune , ever breeding wars in your hardhearted soul . But such strife

sections.label: Section 3.220
sections.translation: should not be pushed into a conflict with the powerful .

sections.label: Section 3.221
sections.translation: I have been forced to it , forced by a terrible compulsion . I know my own passion ; it does not escape me ; but , under such terrible compulsion , I will not restrain these disastrous cries of frenzy ,

sections.label: Section 3.225
sections.translation: so long as life is in me . Who indeed , my noble friends , who that keeps what is appropriate in mind , would think any word of comfort right for my ears ? Let me be , let me be , my comforters !

sections.label: Section 3.230
sections.translation: For these ills will be reckoned with those which have no cure ; I will never know a respite from my pains , or the sum of this wailing .
